archetypes.name: ZARDSUN
archetypes.prevalence: The dominant archetype: ~3 out of 8 lists at recent tournaments
howItWorks: Mega Zard → Drought. Sun = Fire +50%, opposing Water −50%, instant Solar Beam.
Around it: Chlorophyll (Venusaur) or Unburden (Sneasler) for speed, Whimsicott Tailwind/Encore.
Scarf Garchomp as a sun-independent physical wing (and Rock Slide for mirror Zards).
Floette-M as the slow win condition, Basculegion as the cleaner.
howToBeat: Rock Slide on Zard (Garchomp/Tyranitar/Aerodactyl/Kleavor/Lycanroc).
WEATHER WAR: bring in your own setter AFTER the Mega (sun lasts 5 turns; the last weather wins).
Thermal Exchange Baxcalibur (Fire moves pump it up).
Bulky Waters once the sun is down.
Taunt the Tailwind setters.
archetypes.notes: Sun also boosts opposing Fire-types: mirrors become weather-timing races.

archetypes.name: Goodstuff / Incineroar Balance
archetypes.prevalence: The style of the Turin winner (Marco Silva, 1st of 941)
howItWorks: No weather. Double Mega option (chosen at preview).
Fake Out + Intimidate + Parting Shot for positioning control.
Kingambit as the endgame anchor.
OPTIONAL Perish engine (Gengar + Soundproof Kommo-o): two interchangeable win conditions.
howToBeat: Punish the passivity (protected setup).
Fairy ×4 on Kommo-o, Fighting ×4 on Kingambit.
Keep the tempo high: their advantage grows late-game with Supreme Overlord.
archetypes.notes: The hybrid is the lesson of the Regional: flexible win conditions beat monolithic plans.

archetypes.name: Rain
howItWorks: Rain = Water +50%, opposing Fire −50%, 100% accurate Hurricane.
Archaludon's Electro Shot with no charge turn (+1 SpA on every use).
Adaptability Basculegion is devastating in rain.
"Control" version: dual Tailwind/Trick Room mode (Sinistcha TR + Sableye screens/Quash).
howToBeat: Weather war (bring your setter in after theirs).
Electric ×4 on Pelipper, Grass on Politoed.
Deny Archaludon tempo: KO it within 2 turns or it snowballs.
Encore the setters.
archetypes.notes: Reference list: Cybertron, replica code UBVEGRW250.

archetypes.name: Perish Trap (full-trap)
archetypes.prevalence: Polarizing: extremely strong vs the unprepared, weak when counter-picked
howItWorks: Mega Gengar = SHADOW TAG: opponents cannot switch.
Perish Song = a counter of 3 on everything on the field; at 0, faint. YOUR mons reset it by switching, theirs can't.
Soundproof Kommo-o = immune to its ally's song = a win condition that stays on the field.
Disable/Encore shut down the answers; Politoed = second singer.
Counter rule: Gengar switches out on the turn ITS OWN count hits 1 (opponents trapped at move selection stay locked in → they die, it escapes).
howToBeat: GHOSTS = immune to trapping (Basculegion, 51% usage!).
Pivot moves bypass the cage (U-turn/Volt Switch/Flip Turn/Parting Shot) and reset the count.
Prankster Taunt on the singer.
Throat Chop (silences sound moves for 2 turns).
Magic Bounce (Hatterene) REFLECTS the song; Good as Gold is immune.
The double KO from Perish CHARGES the opponent's Last Respects/Supreme Overlord.
Counter-pick at preview: the trapper telegraphs the plan → know when to leave Gengar on the bench.
archetypes.notes: All countermeasures field-tested. Lesson: this is why the winner runs the hybrid (goodstuff-incineroar).

archetypes.name: Snow double-Mega
howItWorks: Froslass-M = snow + Aurora Veil (damage halved for 5 turns).
Behind screens, frail mons (Sneasler/Lycanroc) become sturdy.
Double Mega option at preview.
howToBeat: Screens do NOT stop status/Perish/Encore.
Fire on the Ice-types; Steel/Rock on Froslass; Iron Head ×4 on Ninetales-A (variant).
Steal the weather.

archetypes.name: Trick Room
archetypes.prevalence: Always present in the meta, rarely full TR
howItWorks: Typical setters: Hatterene (Magic Bounce!), Indeedee-F (blocks priority + Follow Me), Oranguru (Instruct too), Farigiraf (Armor Tail, can't be Faked Out), Sinistcha.
Typical abusers: Ursaluna, Torkoal (Eruption), Golurk-M, Steelix-M, Snorlax, Mr. Rime.
howToBeat: Taunt BEFORE the setup (but: Magic Bounce reflects it, Armor Tail blocks priority Taunts).
Fake Out the setter (but Indeedee/Farigiraf neutralize it).
Encore the Trick Room.
Survive the 5 turns with Protect/switches.
Remember: TR does NOT change priority (priority brackets still go first).

archetypes.name: Minor archetypes
howItWorks: Baton Pass (Speed Boost Espathra → passes the boosts) — fragile to Taunt/phazing.
Hybrid Perish-rain.
Delphox-M goodstuff (2nd-3rd at Turin).
Sand (Tyranitar-M + Sand Rush Excadrill).

microCores.name: Fake Out + setter
microCores.how: Fake Out covers the setup turn.

microCores.name: Helping Hand + nuke
microCores.how: Boosted Jet Punch = OHKO even on neutral targets.

microCores.name: Prankster Tailwind + frail hitters
microCores.how: Prankster = guaranteed Tailwind on turn one.

microCores.name: Intimidate-cycling
microCores.how: Parting Shot → comes back in → fresh Intimidate, on a loop.
counters: Clear Amulet (if in the pool: unconfirmed)

microCores.name: Unburden + consumable item
microCores.how: Item consumed → Speed ×2 permanently while on the field. CC drops its defenses → White Herb restores them and is consumed → automatic combo.

microCores.name: Last Respects engine
microCores.how: Save it for last: each fallen ally = +50 power (at 2 fallen = 150, devastating with Adaptability).

microCores.name: Supreme Overlord anchor
microCores.how: ALWAYS the last of the 4: +20% Atk per fallen ally.

microCores.name: Instruct
microCores.how: The partner immediately repeats its attack = 2 hits/turn (e.g. double Brave Bird).

microCores.name: Quash + attacker
microCores.how: The opposing redirector/threat acts last that turn (anti-redirect tech).

microCores.name: Double Perish singer
microCores.how: A single Throat Chop/Taunt can't silence both.

microCores.name: Redirect + setup
microCores.how: Rage Powder/Follow Me protect the setter on the key turn.

teambuildingPrinciples: Every competitive team has: a speed engine (weather ability/Tailwind/TR) + glue (Fake Out/Intimidate/redirection) + a late-game anchor + an answer to Basculegion + a plan for Fairies.
ONE identity only: "split" teams (half fast, half slow) sabotage themselves.
Pick the Mega for the missing ROLE, not for damage: the real constraint is the Mega slot.
Item clause (each item once per team) → distribute Sash/berries by frailty priority.
The signal piece visible at preview warps the opponent's picks: it provides value even from the bench.
